summary: Comprehensive suite covering all task requirements: cartesian product generation, include/exclude rules, max-parallel, fail-fast, config-level max-combinations override, and size validation. Edge cases are well-represented — partial exclude matching, include augmenting vs. appending, scalar (non-array) dimensions, three-dimensional products, and combined exclude+include semantics. CLI integration tests using fixture files add valuable end-to-end coverage. Minor gaps: no tests for empty include/exclude arrays, rules referencing non-existent dimension keys, or truly empty dimension values; the 'Empty dimensions' context contains a test labeled 'throws for empty configuration' that actually expects success — a misleading description. Fixture-based tests introduce external file dependencies that could break in isolation. Design is clean idiomatic Pester with logical Context grouping and readable inline config fixtures, though a BeforeEach/AfterEach cleanup phase is absent (not critical since configs are local variables).
